message_text: I do want to clear this loan, but I cannot pay the full amount today.

I’m an AI agent acting on behalf of Nira, and this conversation is being logged. I’m contacting you about your Nira loan ending ****4831. To verify I’m speaking with the right person, please confirm the last four digits of the account reference.
Thank you. Your current amount due is 12921. What amount can you pay today?
I can pay 2000 today. I had an unexpected family medical expense and my salary is delayed, but I can pay more after my next salary credit.
I understand there has been a family medical expense and a salary delay. You can pay 2000 today and more after your next salary credit. I’m handing this to the next agent to review available options.

Thanks. I can make the 2000 payment now, and after my salary comes in I should be able to pay more—please let me know the realistic plan options.

Given the hardship noted on your account, the realistic policy-compliant path is a hardship review for either a structured payment plan or reduced closure. I can confirm one boundary from the available policy data: any plan must complete within 30 days and cannot extend beyond that. Based on what you’ve already stated, if you can make 2000 now, can you complete the remaining balance within 30 days of today?
Yes, I should be able to clear the remaining balance within 30 days if I pay 2000 now and the rest after my salary is credited. Please share the exact dates and amounts you can approve.

I can confirm the current policy boundary, but I cannot confirm exact approved dates and amounts from the available data in this turn. For Nira, any structured plan must complete within 30 days, and reduced closure also requires approval. I’ve recorded your proposal as 2000 now and the remaining balance by 2026-04-20 for hardship review. Because you asked for exact approvable terms, the next step is lender review of that request. Given the contact restriction already on the case, you should only be contacted if an approved option is available.
